title: Gem scaffold: gemspec, lib/ structure, Rakefile, README
RSpec/Minitest test helpers + full test suite
Actor resolution: thread-local AuditTail.actor= with controller concern
AuditTail.log() for custom events with structured metadata
Production-grade audit_tail
description: Make audit_tail safe and efficient for high-traffic production Rails apps. Key concerns: synchronous cloud sync blocking request threads, no HTTP connection pooling, unbounded table growth, no batching.

title: Move cloud sync off the request thread
description: CloudSync.call runs synchronously in AR callbacks and Log.call, blocking Puma workers for up to 15s per event. Replace with an ActiveJob-based adapter (default: inline for backwards compat, async when configured). Provide a config option: config.cloud_sync_adapter = :inline | :active_job | :sidekiq. The job should accept a serialized event ID and re-fetch + POST.

title: HTTP connection pooling for cloud sync
description: Net::HTTP opens a new TCP connection per event. Use a persistent connection (Net::HTTP#start with keep-alive) or switch to Faraday/httpx with a connection pool. Must be thread-safe for Puma. Add a config option for pool size.

title: Batch cloud sync events
description: Currently one HTTP POST per audit event. Buffer events (in-process queue or Redis list) and flush in batches (e.g. up to 100 events or every 5s). The cloud API endpoint already accepts arrays. Reduces HTTP overhead dramatically under load.

title: Table partitioning / pruning strategy
description: audit_events grows unboundedly. Add: (1) a built-in retention policy (config.retention_days) that runs via a rake task or scheduled job to DELETE old rows; (2) document Postgres declarative partitioning by created_at for large deployments; (3) add a migration generator for a partial index on (subject_type, subject_id) and (actor_type, actor_id) to keep query performance acceptable at scale.

title: Fiber-safety audit for actor storage
description: Actor uses Thread.current (with a fiber-local fallback). On Falcon or other fiber-based servers this leaks state between requests on the same thread. Audit all actor read/write paths and ensure the storage key is fiber-local (Fiber[:audit_tail_actor]) with a Thread fallback for Puma/Unicorn. Add CI matrix entry for a fiber-concurrent scenario.

title: Structured error handling and observability
description: CloudSync silently rescues all StandardError with no logging, making failures invisible. Add: instrumentation via ActiveSupport::Notifications (audit_tail.cloud_sync.error, audit_tail.event.write), optional structured logging via config.logger, and a circuit-breaker or retry-with-backoff for transient cloud sync failures. Expose a health-check method.

title: audit_trail macro: create/update/destroy callbacks with changeset diffs
feat: cloud sync backend for audit_tail gem
description:
Add cloud sync support to the audit_tail gem so events written locally are also POSTed to audit_tail_cloud.

What's needed:
1. config.cloud_api_key and config.cloud_sync_url options in Configuration
2. HTTP backend (lib/audit_tail/backends/http.rb) that maps AuditTail::Event attributes to the cloud payload shape
3. CloudSyncJob (ActiveJob) that batches and POSTs events to POST /api/v1/events with bearer auth
4. Hook the backend into ModelCallbacks and Log so events are synced after local write
5. Tests

Cloud API: POST /api/v1/events, Authorization: Bearer <key>, body: {events: [...]}, max 1000/batch
Payload fields: action, actor_type, actor_id, actor_display, subject_type, subject_id, subject_display, changeset, metadata, occurred_at, environment
